name: Biscotti Cioccolato
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Instant espresso or coffee powder
recipeInstructions: Preheat oven to 375°F and toast almonds in a wide, shallow pan in the center of the oven, stirring once for 13 minutes. Set aside to cool. Adjust two racks to divide the oven into thirds and reduce temperature to 300°F. Line two cookie sheets with aluminum foil, shiny side up, and set aside. In a small bowl, beat the eggs with the brown sugar and vanilla and almond extracts. Sift together into the large bowl of an electric mixer the flour, baking powder, salt, pepper, ginger, cocoa, espresso, and granulated sugar. Place the chocolate on a cutting board and shred/chop it fine with a sharp knife. Place the cut chocolate in a food processor. Add about 1/2 cup of sifted dry ingredients and process for 1 minute until the chocolate is fine & powdery. Add the chocolate mixture and egg mixture to the dry ingredients. Beat on low speed, scraping the bowl as necessary. Slowly beat in the nuts. Lightly flour a work surface and turn the dough out onto it. Lightly sift flour over the dough. Cut the dough in half. Turn one piece so that it is completely dusted with flour. Form elongated ovals about 10 inches long. Place on baking sheets. They may break apart, so just reshape them. Each oval should be 3" wide in the middle and 1/2-3/4" high. Bake 2 sheets at a time for 50 minutes, reversing sheets top to bottom and front to back once during baking. Remove from oven. Reduce oven temperature to 275°F. Peel away foil. With a serrated bread knife, cut 2/3 - 3/4" diagonal strips. Place them cut side up on baking sheets. Turn biscotti upside down and continue to bake until dry (about 15-20 minutes). Do not overbake. Cool and store in airtight tins. Makes about 30 cookies.
